span.p: NO - adding an optional field, a new endpoint, a new enum value clients ignore

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: YES - it can break a conforming client

span.p: Public/partner - cannot coordinate a flag day

span.p: Does the change remove/rename a field, tighten a type, change semantics, or make an optional input required?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Additive - NO version bump. Ship it; document it.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Can you support old + new behavior simultaneously for a transition window?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: New major version; deprecate the old with Deprecation+Sunset

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the consumer base public/partner, or internal/coordinated?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Version in the URI path - /v2 - most visible, cache-friendly

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Version via media type or a version header - cleaner URLs, needs discipline
